title: Goat Rope
description: You have a fence post located at the point (x, y) in the plane, to which a goat is tethered by a rope. You also have a house, which you model as an axis-aligned rectangle with diagonally opposite corners at the points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2). You want to pick a length of rope that guarantees the goat cannot reach the house. Determine the minimum distance from the fence post to the house, so that you can make sure to use a shorter rope.
input:
The input consists of a single line containing six space-separated integers x, y, x1, y1, x2, and y2, each in the range [−999, 999].

It is guaranteed that x1 < x2 and y1 < y2, and that (x, y) is strictly outside the axis-aligned rectangle with corners at (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).
output: Print the minimum distance from the goat’s post to the house, with a relative or absolute error no more than 0.001.
source: , ICPC, Regionals, North America, Mid-Central Regional, 2018 Mid-Central Regional Programming Contest, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Southeast USA Regional, 2018 Southeast USA Regional Programming Contest, Division 2, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Pacific Northwest Regional, 2018 Pacific Northwest Region Programming Contest, Division 1, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Pacific Northwest Regional, 2018 Pacific Northwest Region Programming Contest, Division 2
